steps.title: Add the equations
steps.content: The system is arranged so the $y$-terms cancel if you add the two equations: $$(x+3)+(x-3)=(-2y+5)+(2y+7).$$

steps.title: Simplify directly to the target
steps.content: On the left, $(x+3)+(x-3)=2x$. On the right, $-2y+2y=0$ and $5+7=12$. Therefore, $$2x=12.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The question asks for $2x$, so there is no need to solve for $x$ or $y$. The value is $12$, which matches choice C. Choice B is the trap from giving $x$ instead of $2x$.
